Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists EcCPS1 as 'NCBI Gene ID: 944811'.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Paper refers to E. coli cyclopropane synthase (EcCPS); this supports cyclopropane synthase/cyclopropane fatty acid synthase.

Source_Species.reason: EcCPS is explicitly from E. coli.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Summary and methods explicitly name 'Escherichia coli cyclopropane synthase'.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study species is Camelina sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Results repeatedly state experiments were done in fad2/fae1 camelina background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: EcCPS expression led to accumulation of cyclopropane fatty acid, identified as 19:0 CPA in lipid analyses.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary states CPAs are feedstocks for coatings, lubricants, plastics and cosmetics.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show CPA in seed oil fractions including PC, PE, DAG, TAG, and tissue analyses/MALDI show embryo axis and cotyledon distribution.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Paper focuses on cyclic/cyclopropane fatty acid accumulation within seed lipid metabolism.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results and discussion show CPA formed on phospholipids PC/PE and then transferred into DAG/TAG.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Paper states EcCPS converts monoenes/18:1 esterified in phospholipids to CPA; in camelina oleic acid decreases while CPA increases in PC and PE.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results indicate 18:1-esterified FA is substrate, and data suggest EcCPS recognizes both PC and PE in plants.

Primary_Product.reason: Product is CPA in esterified lipid species; manuscript reports CPA-containing PC, PE, DAG, and TAG.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: EcCPS is the enzyme creating CPA from 18:1 on phospholipids, i.e. the entry step for CPA formation.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: EcCPS directly produces the target CPA.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion/conclusions explicitly identify downstream bottlenecks as CPA-PC to CPA-DAG and CPA-DAG to CPA-TAG, while EcCPS is the enabling production step.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show EcCPS alone produced up to ~10% total seed FA as CPA; with SfLPAT, >15%; CPA high in PC/PE and lower in DAG/TAG.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: EcCPS was expressed in seeds under phaseolin promoter, and Figure S4 is described as showing equal expression in embryo axis and cotyledon.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results state CPS genes were placed under the seed-specific phaseolin promoter.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: As EcCPS is a cyclopropane synthase/CPS, membership in cyclopropane fatty acid synthase family is supported at a general level.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify camelina growth at 22°C, 16-h photoperiod under transgenic expression.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in paper: transgenic expression, GC-MS, qRT-PCR, lipid separation, stereospecific analysis, ESI-MS/MS, MALDI-MSI.

Omics_Data.reason: Paper reports lipidomics analyses through LC/MS and MALDI/MSI; no accession is given.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: EcCPS was co-expressed with SfLPAT, but direct physical/protein interaction was not demonstrated.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results report <10% seed weight decrease in most lines, mild delay in seedling establishment/growth at high CPA, and no substantial decrease in total fatty acid content or germination in camelina.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion/conclusions present EcCPS as effective for engineering CPA production and suggest stacking with acyltransferases to improve flux to TAG.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly reflected in results: only EcCPS among tested CPS genes produced CPA; CPA >60% in PC/PE but much lower in DAG/TAG; CPA enriched at sn-1 and higher in embryo axis than cotyledon.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state GhCPS1, GhCPS2, and SfCPS gave no detectable CPA, and later sections report elongated 21:0/23:0 CPA and trace desaturated 19:1 CPA in EcCPS lines.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists SfLPAT as 'GenBank: KC894726.1'.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Summary and throughout paper call SfLPAT 'Sterculia foetida lysophosphatidic acid acyltransferase'.

Source_Species.reason: SfLPAT is explicitly from Sterculia foetida.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Paper explicitly gives the Latin name Sterculia foetida.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The transgenic host is Camelina sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Co-expression was performed in fad2/fae1 camelina background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: SfLPAT is linked experimentally to increased cyclopropane fatty acid accumulation when co-expressed with EcCPS.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states CPAs are industrial feedstocks for coatings, lubricants, plastics and cosmetics.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show co-expression increases di-CPA-PC and is associated with increased CPA in DAG and TAG in seed oil.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: SfLPAT is discussed in context of TAG assembly and modified fatty acid incorporation into glycerolipids.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Discussion links LPAT to Kennedy pathway acylation and a CPA acyl exchange cycle increasing di-CPA-PC.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Paper defines LPAT function as transferring an acyl group from acyl-CoA to LPA to form phosphatidic acid, and infers SfLPAT preference for CPA-LPA/CPA-CoA increasing di-CPA lipids.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Methods/discussion describe LPAT substrate as LPA plus acyl-CoA; discussion specifically infers use of CPA-CoA and CPA-LPA.

Primary_Product.reason: Formation of phosphatidic acid is stated for LPAT generally, but 'enhanced di-CPA-containing glycerolipid intermediates' is an inference from downstream lipid data rather than direct product measurement of SfLPAT itself.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: LPAT is a midstream acyltransfer step in glycerolipid/TAG biosynthesis affecting flux into storage lipids.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: SfLPAT does not synthesize CPA directly but promotes its incorporation into DAG/TAG; this is supported by the co-expression data.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Paper presents SfLPAT as helping relieve a bottleneck for CPA incorporation, but not the sole bottleneck since downstream PC→DAG and DAG→TAG steps remain limiting.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show up to 90% increase in CPA versus EcCPS alone in highest line, roughly doubled CPA in DAG and TAG in representative line, and increased di-CPA-PC proportion.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: SfLPAT was expressed in seeds under phaseolin promoter and Figure S4 is described as showing equal expression in embryo axis and cotyledon.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results state SfLPAT was under the control of the seed-specific phaseolin promoter.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify growth at 22°C with 16-h photoperiod for these transgenic camelina plants.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Co-expression, qRT-PCR, GC-MS, stereospecific analyses, ESI-MS/MS, and MALDI-MSI are all used and described.

Omics_Data.reason: Paper includes lipidomics analyses by LC/MS and MALDI/MSI, with no accession reported.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SfLPAT was co-expressed with EcCPS, but no direct molecular interaction between the proteins was shown.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results note slight decrease in CPA in some T4 co-expression lines versus T3, and mild delay in germination/early growth at high CPA, but no major oil or yield penalty.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly argues source-species acyltransferases like SfLPAT are valuable stacking partners to mitigate bottlenecks in modified oil engineering.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported: co-expression raised CPA to >15% and nearly 20%; increased di-CPA-PC and redistributed CPA toward sn-2; increased CPA-DAG and CPA-TAG.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion infers SfLPAT preference for CPA-LPA and CPA-CoA and states increased CPA was metabolic rather than due to elevated EcCPS transcript levels in co-expression lines.
